findings.evidence: `nl -ba .../SplitTorus.lean` shows `theorem pointsMulEquiv_symm_apply` at line 80 without `@[simp]`; `rg -n "@\[simp\]|theorem pointsMulEquiv_symm_apply"` shows the analogous lemmas in `DiagonalizableGroup.lean`, `MultiplicativeGroup.lean`, and `AdditiveGroup.lean` are preceded by `@[simp]`.
findings.issue: `pointsMulEquiv_symm_apply` is the inverse-characterization lemma for the public equivalence, but unlike the analogous `pointsMulEquiv_symm_apply` lemmas it is not tagged `@[simp]`, so `simp` will not normalize constructed split-torus points.

findings.evidence: `nl -ba .../FreeAbelianCharacter.lean` shows `theorem freeAbelianCharEquiv_comp` at line 109 without `@[simp]`, while the surrounding characteristic lemmas at lines 86, 93, and 100 are tagged `@[simp]`.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to `freeAbelianCharEquiv_comp`; the rewrite is oriented to the normal form `ψ (freeAbelianCharEquiv χ i)` and should not loop.
findings.issue: `freeAbelianCharEquiv_comp` is the target-naturality characteristic lemma, but it is not tagged `@[simp]`, so post-composed characters do not reduce to coordinatewise post-composition automatically.

summary: The split-torus and free-character APIs expose the needed characteristic lemmas, but two normal-form lemmas are not registered for automation.
